rules: [P1] Verify patient identity via tool lookup before confirming the association of clinical data.
[P1] Decompose multi-part clinical requests into a sequence of distinct tool calls: retrieve data, evaluate thresholds, and execute dependent orders.
[P1] Distinguish between reporting a requirement and performing the action; always invoke the corresponding tool for state-changing operations.
[P1] Verify the presence of necessary data before proceeding to conditional ordering steps.
[P1] Retrieve current system date via tool call for all temporal arithmetic; state the retrieved date and calculated delta in the reasoning trace.
[P1] When a conditional workflow results in no action, explicitly state the criteria that were not met rather than assuming completion.
[P1] Always perform a targeted search using all provided demographic anchors (Name, DOB) before concluding a record lookup.
[P1] Explicitly state absence of data only after exhausting search strategies (multi-modal, secondary validation, breadth-first expansion).
[P1] Optimize queries using clinical codes (LOINC/SNOMED) and server-side filtering to minimize payload processing.
[P1] Enforce tool-based retrieval for all personal health information; strictly prohibit internal knowledge recall for patient-specific data.
[P1] Document provenance for all retrieved data, including tool references and metadata, to validate constraints.
[P1] Always invoke the specific record-update tool for clinical data entry; do not treat user input as a completed state change.
[P1] Ensure all clinical queries include explicit temporal parameters (start/end timestamps) to align with task-specific lookback requirements.
[P1] Ensure the final response explicitly references the tool output confirming the data persistence.
[P1] Map data into target schemas strictly ensuring all mandatory formatting components are present.
[P1] Prioritize tool execution over simulation; verify output structure and presence before synthesizing final answers.
[P1] Validate the existence and integrity of retrieved data before finalizing any numerical output or clinical decision.
[P1] Verify tool execution success via output confirmation before asserting that a dependent action has been completed.
[P1] Explicitly handle 'null' or empty search results by returning the exact error string defined in the task instructions.
[P1] Execute conditional logic as a strict gate: verify and state the evaluation result before initiating dependent actions.
[P1] Draft a complete execution plan including all conditional steps before initiating the first data retrieval.
[P1] Include patient identifiers (MRN, name) and ISO-8601 timestamps in all confirmation messages for auditability.
[P1] Prioritize temporal constraint verification: if the timeframe is invalid, terminate the workflow immediately.
